misconceptionSummary: Students take out a common factor but do not divide every term correctly inside the bracket.

steps.narration: This factorisation is wrong because the terms inside the bracket have not both been divided by four.
If four is taken out, eight x divided by four becomes two x.
And twelve divided by four becomes three.
So the correct factorisation is four bracket two x plus three.

visuals.expression: Every original term must be divided by the factor taken out
visuals.label: Check both terms inside the bracket

steps.narration: To avoid the mistake, test each term separately when building the bracket.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: taking out a factor but leaving one inside term unchanged. If a factor is taken out, every term must be divided by it.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: A factor outside the bracket affects the whole expression, so every term inside must be adjusted to match it.
